title: convolvulus sibthorpii
intro: Convolvulus sibthorpii, also known as the 'small bindweed' or 'small calabar', is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to induce vomiting and relieve digestive issues. The plant's roots and leaves contain alkaloids with emetic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Convolvulus sibthorpii are made from its roots and leaves, and can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and dried powders, offering potential health benefits when used under medical supervision.
